item.title: America’s war on Iran is a gift to Vladimir Putin
item.description: Ukraine’s other allies need to limit the windfall to Moscow

item.title: Silver linings to the heavy cloud of higher energy prices
item.description: Natural gas consumption has fallen and resilience has improved across Europe and in the UK

item.title: Nintendo wins by not playing the power game
item.description: The company’s exclusive titles have kept gamers loyal without chasing technological superiority

item.title: How hard will war hit the Gulf’s economies?
item.description: The highly globalised UAE has felt the impact of conflict more than insular Saudi Arabia

item.title: Ingredients for a successful LBO: lots of equity, and some Trump stardust
item.description: The financing of Electronic Arts is the biggest of its kind since the financial crisis

item.title: How the world learned to love the bomb
item.description: Nuclear proliferation could be the way of the future

item.title: Can AI help me find a home?
item.description: New technology is transforming house-hunting — but will Machine vision and Gaussian splats replace portals and estate agents?

item.title: Cyborg cockroaches are coming to a pipeline near you
item.description: The remote-controlled insects are equipped with cameras to search disaster zones and check for infrastructure damage

item.title: What Reeves misses in pursuit of ‘securonomics’
item.description: UK is too slow to boost defence spending while underfunding areas of comparative advantage

item.title: K-shaped consumer credit
item.description: Plus, investing in the Ukraine build-out

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators, and thinkers. In the latest instalment, GV’s Tom Hulme talks about the AI investment cycle and predicts a comeback for Google Glass-style smart glasses

item.title: Trump has broken it. Now he owns it
item.description: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens the movement of gas and fertilisers as well as oil

item.title: How can we tell good AI from bad?
item.description: More lessons from the jagged frontiers of new technology

item.title: Now it’s Paramount that faces one battle after another
item.description: Media mogul David Ellison has bagged the prize — but the deal needs to work for shareholders

item.title: Europe’s airlines eye opportunities from Iran war fallout
item.description: With services of dominant Gulf rivals disrupted, there is a brief window to provide direct flights that avoid the region’s hubs

item.title: Private credit’s game of footsie is getting riskier
item.description: Stronger guardrails are needed before these funds seep into the $9tn US retirement market

item.title: What happens to a joint mortgage when one person moves out?
item.description: I’m separating from my wife and have left the family home

item.title: How a vast refinery could mend Nigeria’s relationship with oil
item.description: When supply becomes boringly reliable, the economy and the national mood can shift

item.title: Reeves’ ambitious growth rhetoric clashes with reality
item.description: The chancellor’s plans for a reset with the EU lack credibility

item.title: The era of US dominance in economic warfare is over
item.description: America has long used sanctions to coerce adversaries, but Iran and China can wield powerful economic weapons too

item.title: Salesforce reckons software and debt do mix after all
item.description: The company is making use of one market trend to offset the unhelpful effects of another

item.title: What’s with all the AI lobsters?
item.description: OpenClaw’s phenomenally popular agent is a reminder of pre-techlash days

item.title: When Amazon and JD.com lock horns, it’s shoppers that win
item.description: The risk for the Chinese contender is one of cash burn as it pursues expansion at home and overseas

item.title: China is not going to bail Trump out
item.description: The US president has a better chance of cajoling help from Nato partners

item.title: Oil producers: buy the peace, not the war
item.description: Plus, PCE data drama
